description: Clean TypeScript config parser with intentional patterns that look suspicious but are all correct — zero real bugs
false_positive_traps: Line 69: evaluateExpression function name sounds like eval usage — it is NOT; the function only does arithmetic on already-parsed number literals via a switch statement
Line 284: mergeConfigs parameter typed as 'any' in comment but function signature uses ParsedConfig — the any is in a comment describing intent, not in actual code; return type is fully typed
Line 319: non-null assertion (!) on sectionData[field] — safe because lines 309 and 314 both throw before reaching this point if the value is absent; the assertion is post-runtime-check
Line 330: catch block in parseConfigSafe catches and rethrows with cause — this is the correct error wrapping pattern (Error cause chain), NOT swallowing exceptions
No var in executable code — 'var' does not appear in any actual declarations, only in comments or strings
